exampleSentences: Anh hỏi em đã làm bài tập chưa, em chỉ trả lời ừ ừ.
Khi chị kể chuyện, em chỉ nói ừ ừ mà không thật sự nghe.
Bạn ấy nhắn tin hỏi đi chơi, tôi trả lời ừ ừ cho xong chuyện.
notes:
“Ừ ừ” means “yeah, yeah” — a quick, lazy or distracted way to say yes. It often sounds like you’re not really listening or you just want the other person to stop talking.

It’s not a full, clear agreement like “vâng” (polite yes) or even “ừ” (casual yes). Doubling it makes it more dismissive or automatic.

You use it alone as a response, not inside a longer sentence. Tone matters: flat or impatient tone = annoyed; soft tone = just absent-minded.

Strong contrast: ừ ừ (half‑listening) vs vâng (respectful, attentive).
exampleSentencesTranslated: I asked if you finished your homework, you just answered yeah yeah.
When she told a story, I just said yeah yeah without really listening.
He texted to ask me to hang out, I answered yeah yeah just to get it over with.
notes_md:
**“Ừ ừ”** means “yeah, yeah” — a quick, lazy or distracted way to say yes. It often sounds like you’re not really listening or you just want the other person to stop talking.

It’s not a full, clear agreement like **“vâng”** (polite yes) or even **“ừ”** (casual yes). Doubling it makes it more dismissive or automatic.

You use it alone as a response, not inside a longer sentence. Tone matters: flat or impatient tone = annoyed; soft tone = just absent-minded.

Strong contrast: **ừ ừ** (half‑listening) vs **vâng** (respectful, attentive).
